---
format: html
title: get_scale_id_from_store()
---

<html><head>
<meta http-equiv="Content-Type" content="text/html; charset=utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, user-scalable=yes" />
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.css">
<script type="text/javascript">
const macros = { "\\R": "\\textsf{R}", "\\mbox": "\\text", "\\code": "\\texttt"};
function processMathHTML() {
    var l = document.getElementsByClassName('reqn');
    for (let e of l) { katex.render(e.textContent, e, { throwOnError: false, macros }); }
    return;
}</script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.js"
    onload="processMathHTML();"></script>
<link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="R.css" />
</head><body><div class="container"><main>

<table style="width: 100%;"><tr><td>get_scale_id_from_store</td><td style="text-align: right;">R Documentation</td></tr></table>

<h2>Get Scale ID from Current Targets Store Path</h2>

<h3>Description</h3>

<p>Determines whether the currently active targets store corresponds
to a spatial unit by inspecting the store path and checking it
against the project's spatial grid CSV catalogue. Returns the
'scale_id' string for spatial pipelines, or 'NULL' for
non-spatial (named-project) pipelines.
</p>


<h3>Usage</h3>

<pre><code class='language-R'>get_scale_id_from_store(
  store = targets::tar_path_store(),
  file = here::here("Data/Input/spatial_grid.csv")
)
</code></pre>


<h3>Arguments</h3>

<table role = "presentation">
<tr><td><code id="store">store</code></td>
<td>
<p>A single character string giving the path to the targets data
store. Default: 'targets::tar_path_store()', which resolves
correctly both inside target commands (including callr worker
processes) and when called interactively.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="file">file</code></td>
<td>
<p>Path to the spatial grid CSV catalogue file.
Default: 'here::here(&quot;Data/Input/spatial_grid.csv&quot;)'.</p>
</td></tr>
</table>


<h3>Details</h3>

<p>The store path convention for spatial pipelines is:
'target_store/scale_id/pipeline_name/'. The function
extracts the second-to-last path component via
'basename(dirname(store))' and checks whether it appears in the
'scale_id' column of the spatial grid CSV. For non-spatial
pipelines (e.g. 'Data/targets/project_cz/pipeline_basic/') the
candidate ('&quot;project_cz&quot;') is not in the CSV, so 'NULL' is
returned. When the CSV file does not exist the function returns
'NULL' gracefully.
</p>


<h3>Value</h3>

<p>A single character string with the 'scale_id' when the store
path corresponds to a spatial unit in the CSV catalogue, or
'NULL' otherwise.
</p>


<h3>See Also</h3>

<p>get_spatial_window, get_spatial_model_params
</p>

</main>

</div>
</body></html>
